Plaintiff???S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order That The Truth Of Each Matter Specified In Plaintiff???S Requests For Admission (Set One) Be Deemed Admitted, Or In The Alternative, To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff???S Request For Admission And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff???S Judicial Council Form Interrogatories (Set One); And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
William Ruiz, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Al Raia
Stephen E. Carlson, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Plant Insulation Co.
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Al Raia's Motion For An Order That The Truth Of Matters Specified In Plaintiff's Requests For Admission (Set One) Be Deemed Admitted, or in the alternative, To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Requests For Admission; Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Judicial Council Form Interrogatories (Set One) is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those supplemental responses on this motion. However, counsel are advised that the court would not find merit to the objections to the 17.1 Form Interrogatory which assert that the interrogatory is vague, ambiguous, unduly burdensome, harassing, and irrelevant. The court also finds no merit in the response as it asserts there is undue burden in setting forth facts supporting the responses or any other evidence presumably in the possession of Plaintiff's attorneys. The reservation of an alleged right to present witnesses supportive of various responses based upon facts obtained or analysis made subsequent to the propounding of these interrogatories would be ordered stricken. There is no authority allowing general objections.
The court finds that the initial responses were made without substantial justification. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant in the amount of $565, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
